title: SQL Injection (SQLi)
description: Injecting malicious SQL code into input fields to manipulate database queries.
fullExplanation: SQL Injection occurs when user-supplied input is incorporated into SQL queries without proper sanitization. An attacker can manipulate the query to bypass authentication, extract data, modify records, or execute OS commands. It remains the #1 web vulnerability for decades. Types include Classic (error-based), Blind (boolean/time-based), and Out-of-band SQLi.

types.name: Classic / Error-based
types.description: Database error messages reveal schema info. ' OR 1=1-- bypasses login.

types.name: Blind Boolean-based
types.description: No visible output — infer data from true/false responses. ' AND 1=1-- vs ' AND 1=2--

types.name: Blind Time-based
types.description: Use SLEEP() or WAITFOR DELAY to infer data from response timing.

types.name: Union-based
types.description: UNION SELECT to append attacker's query results to legitimate results.

types.name: Out-of-band
types.description: Exfiltrate data via DNS or HTTP requests triggered by the database.

types.name: Second-order
types.description: Payload stored in DB, executed later when retrieved by another query.

attackSteps.description: Test inputs with ' or " — look for SQL errors or behavior changes
Query information_schema.tables to enumerate databases and tables
SELECT username, password FROM users — extract credentials
xp_cmdshell (MSSQL), INTO OUTFILE (MySQL) for OS command execution

keyFacts: OWASP Top 10 #3 (Injection) — most critical web vulnerability category
sqlmap automates detection and exploitation completely
Parameterized queries / prepared statements completely prevent SQLi
Stored procedures don't prevent SQLi unless they use parameterized queries
WAF bypass: encoding, comments, case variation (SeLeCt, /*!SELECT*/)
Time-based blind SQLi works even when no output is returned
Second-order SQLi bypasses input validation at storage time

defenses.name: Parameterized Queries
defenses.description: Use prepared statements — user input never interpreted as SQL code

defenses.name: ORM Frameworks
defenses.description: SQLAlchemy, Hibernate, ActiveRecord use parameterized queries by default

defenses.name: Input Validation
defenses.description: Whitelist allowed characters, reject suspicious patterns

defenses.name: Least Privilege DB
defenses.description: App DB user should only have SELECT/INSERT — not DROP or xp_cmdshell

defenses.name: WAF
defenses.description: Web Application Firewall blocks common SQLi patterns

defenses.name: Error Handling
defenses.description: Never expose DB error messages to users — log internally only
